Rita Pucci (rita.pucci85@gmail.com)
2024-06-19 06:00:24

Italy Udine - Exciting PhD Opportunities in Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence at the University of Udine

The University of Udine is excited to announce three fully funded PhD positions in our Machine Learning and Perception (MLP) team. This is your chance to delve into cutting-edge research in a vibrant academic environment in Italy.
What You'll Be Doing

As a PhD student, you'll be an integral part of our MLP research group, focusing on one of the following innovative projects:

  • SistAnimalID: Develop an advanced animal recognition system.

  • BioSubAcque: Engage in underwater image analysis for environmental monitoring.

  • TrustVision: Create efficient and reliable artificial vision systems for Industry 4.0.

You'll work closely with our team of experienced researchers, participate in regular group meetings, and have the opportunity to present your work at top conferences.
You're Probably a Match If You Have
  • An MSc degree (or expect to obtain one by October 2024) in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field.

  • A strong interest in machine learning, computer vision, or related areas.

  • Good programming skills and familiarity with relevant tools and frameworks.

  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities.

  • The ability to work independently and as part of a team.

About the MLP Research Group

Our group is dedicated to pioneering research in Machine Learning and AI for a variety of applications. With over six members, we have a track record of publishing in prestigious venues and collaborating with industrial and academic partners. We pride ourselves on providing a supportive and stimulating environment where you can thrive and reach your full potential.
A Glimpse of What We Offer
  • Fully Funded Position: Covering tuition fees and a competitive stipend.

  • Research Support: Funding for conference travel and necessary computational materials.

  • Dynamic Location: Based in Udine, a charming city close to the seaside, ski resorts, and cultural hubs like Venice, Austria, Slovenia, and Croatia.

How to Apply

Interested applicants are encouraged to contact me at niki.martinel@uniud.it for more information.
Applications must be submitted through the official portal as outlined in the call for applications by June 20, 2024, 2 PM Italian time.
Don't miss this opportunity to advance your research career at the University of Udine, where academic excellence meets a quality lifestyle.
Please feel free to share this opportunity with anyone who might be interested. We appreciate your help in contacting potential candidates.

Prof. Niki Martinel
University of Udine

Università degli Studi di Udine
Call for Application for admission to the Ph.D. Programmes (40° Cycle)
University of Udine PhD programmes: Law and Innovation in the European Legal Space; Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence; Industrial and Information Engineering; Molecular Medicine; Food Science; Environmental and Energy Engineering Science; Agricultural and Biotechnological Sciences; Mathematical and Physical Sciences; Clinical and Translational Medical Sciences; Art History, Film Studies, Media Studies and Music; Linguistics and Literature. Positions are with or without scholarship. The scholarships are financed by UniUD Resources, by the associated institutions and by external institutions (public or private)including the FVG Region, from ESF+ 2021-2027 PS 22/23 Line A (2024 Edition)resources.

Adriaan Dokter (amd427@cornell.edu)
2024-06-26 16:28:59

2yr Postdoc Opportunity at Cornell Lab of Ornithology, Ithaca, NY
See full listing here. BirdFlow is an innovative, NSF-funded project that uses the Cornell Lab’s eBird database to infer the movements of migratory birds across the annual cycle using innovative AI/machine learning approaches. The Postdoctoral Associate will lead research that utilizes BirdFlow models to expand our knowledge of bird movements, including studying migratory connectivity and its associations with population trends, investigating species’ resilience to climate change and environmental fluctuations, and exploring BirdFlow models for providing real-time information on species’ movements in the context of the BirdCast public website. The postdoc will also work on integrating additional data sources, such as BirdCast radar data, individual tracking data and banding data, to improve and validate BirdFlow models. With the BirdFlow team, the postdoc will have an opportunity to contribute to products and software that will be released for scientists, conservation practitioners, industry, and the public to explore and investigate the phenomenon of avian migration. Join our BirdFlow team with @Dan Sheldon @Benjamin Van Doren

I would like to bring to your attention an open position at the Institute of Applied Mathematics and Information Technologies of the CNR in Genoa (Italy).
https://selezionionline.cnr.it/jconon/call-detail?callCode=400.28%20IMATI%20PNRR
Main topic
“Use and development of models for the simulation of rain-induced erosive processes in scenarios of land cover change, fire events, morphological elements” within the Spoke 5 “Urban biodiversity” of the PNRR NBFC “National Biodiversity Future Center” project, code identifier CN00000033 - CUP: B83C22002930006
Role
Level III researcher
Duration
12 months, extendable up to the maximum duration of the project
Place of work
Institute of Applied Mathematics and Information Technologies “E Magenes” (IMATI) of the CNR - branch of Genoa
Call deadline
July 15, 2024
Requirements
a) Master’s degree or specialist degree, or old degree degree relevant to the topic of the project. For degrees obtained abroad, recognition of the qualification is required. The candidate who is not yet in possession of the recognition provision must declare that he/she has submitted this request in accordance with current legislation within the deadline of the announcement, reporting the transmission details in the curriculum vitae. In this case, the candidate will be admitted to the selection with reservations, without prejudice to the fact that this measure must be presented before signing the employment contract;
b) At least three years’ research experience in geomorphology, pedology or in general geology, environmental sciences, environmental engineering), or possession of the title of Research Doctor or PhD relevant to the required experience;
c) Knowledge of the English language.
For information, please contact ulderico.fugacci@cnr.it

Oisin Mac Aodha (macaodha@caltech.edu)
2024-07-09 04:17:46

Head of Engineering role at iNaturalist
https://app.beapplied.com/apply/gotyyjtuxa?utm_source=x

app.beapplied.com
Head of Engineering
iNaturalist seeks a dynamic and experienced Head of Engineering to join our leadership team. This important role will report directly to the Executive Director and will be pivotal in shaping the future of iNaturalist. The ideal candidate will possess a blend of strategic vision, technical expertise, and team leadership skills to effectively lead our engineering team.About iNaturalistiNaturalist is the leading biodiversity platform on the planet where millions of community scientists, supported by a vast network of expert naturalists, crowdsource one of the world's largest biodiversity databases. With 4 million monthly active users and 400 thousand monthly active contributors, each month iNaturalist generates over 6 million observations of over 100 thousand distinct species. The iNaturalist database is the backbone of conservation science, supports a global network of stewards, and motivates a movement of nature advocates. Launched in 2008 and operating as a nonprofit committed to open data, iNaturalist is at the forefront of combining crowdsourcing and AI models to further our mission. iNaturalist's vision is a world where everyone can understand and sustain biodiversity through the practice of observing wild organisms and sharing information about them. We are an independent 501(c)3 non-profit. ResponsibilitiesOrganizational LeadershipStrategic Planning: Collaborate with the Executive Director, Head of Engagement, and Head of Development to develop and execute organizational strategies, goals, and operating plans.Goal Setting: Contribute to setting and achieving goals that focus on expanding iNaturalist’s impact on biodiversity, growing the community, enhancing the product, building organizational capacity, and ensuring sustainability.Cross-functional Collaboration: Foster a collaborative environment across all departments to ensure alignment and integration of efforts towards common <a href="http://goals.Engineering">goals.Engineering</a> Team LeadershipTeam Leadership: Cultivate a culture of high performance coupled with a celebration of achievement within the engineering team. Develop career paths, conduct performance reviews, and manage recruitment and hiring processes.Technical Oversight: Overseeing a team of 10 engineers, designers, and project managers involved in mobile and web interfaces, database systems, AI models, and backend operations.Workflow: Coordinate and streamline the management of engineering projects, timelines, and launches. Ensure timely delivery of high-quality products.Experience and QualificationsManagement Experience: Extensive experience managing and growing a team of 10+ engineers. Experience managing in a remote environment and experience with open source projects.Technical Expertise: Experience with the following technologies: mobile and web development, database management, AI, and backend operations and software development processes including systems architecture, software design, and testing that cover all phases of traditional software development lifecycle. Experience with agile/scrum, cloud deployments, and managing major vendor relationships (e.g. Google, Azure, Apple)Collaborative Spirit: Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across departments and with diverse stakeholders.Passion for Nature: A strong commitment to nature and technology as well as an interest in science and conservation.Why Join Us?Impact: Play a pivotal role in a mission-driven organization dedicated to biodiversity conservation and community engagement.Innovation: Leading a team of developers building cutting-edge technology that empowers millions of people to make a positive impact on nature.Growth: Be part of a growing organization with opportunities for personal and professional development.If you are passionate about biodiversity, technology, and leading talented teams to achieve meaningful impact, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity.Compensation and BenefitsWhy it’s Great to work at iNaturalist  A mission that matters. With species going extinct daily, the need to protect and document biodiversity has never been more essential. iNaturalist has become the go-to data source to measure biodiversity conservation and protection. A great team.Smart, hard-working nature lovers make up our small team. We live in countries all over the world but come together each day to further our mission. For this position, to facilitate collaboration across time zones, we require that you be a resident of and eligible to work in the lower 48 states (i.e., not Alaska or Hawaii). Flexible work. We are a virtual team, and most of this position’s work can be performed from home or wherever you are comfortable. You’ll even get some funds to set up your office and a monthly stipend to defray some of the costs. Some travel to meetings and events will  be required. Competitive pay.The salary for this full-time position is $182,350 per year, non-negotiable. Great benefits.We offer a pretty awesome benefits package, including medical, dental, vision and life insurance, plus an employer-funded health reimbursement account and employee-funded flexible spending accounts. There is a 401k plan with a 5% match. This position is eligible for unlimited personal time off, and unlike some tech companies, we really mean it – everyone is expected to take a minimum of three weeks a year off. Eligible new parents get up to 12 weeks of paid leave and an additional 18 of unpaid. Application processInstead of a cover letter, we will ask you to answer two questions that will be reviewed (by real people, not AI) and assessed separately from each other, your resume, and your name. The hiring team will review your answers and resume in a batch of answers and resumes from other candidates in randomized orders. Randomization and anonymization of each element in the initial review process minimizes bias.After initial review of applications on a rolling basis, advancing candidates will be asked to answer a few more questions within one week. Process Summary:Application (July)Written follow up (July/Aug)First round interview (Aug)Second round interview (Aug)Offer (Sept)Ideal start date: October 1
Original URL: https://app.beapplied.com/apply/gotyyjtuxa?utm_source=x

Mara Baudena (mara.baudena@cnr.it)
2024-07-29 04:34:25

Post-doc researcher position in modelling grassland biodiversity in CNR Torino, Italy
Position: Assegno di ricerca, postdoc- researcher
12 months (to be later extended till the end of the project, currently 2026-03-31)

Profile description:
Experience in at least one of the following areas: 1) modeling of ecosystems with mathematical and/or numerical models of various complexity, and relative knowledge of programming languages (eg Python, C ++, Matlab, R); 2) Analysis of large ecological databases with advanced analysis techniques (e.g. network, Machine Learning)

Project description:
The post-doc researcher will work within the framework of the DiviN-P project "Protecting plant diversity via stoichiometric nutrient networks across Europe", funded by the European program Biodiversa+. The project is led by prof. Martin Wassen (Utrecht University, the Netherlands), and includes as project partners the groups of prof. Susana Bautista (Univ of Alicante, Spain) and of prof. Harry OldeVenterink (Univ. of Ghent, Belgium), together with the group of Dr. Mara Baudena at CNR-ISAC where the postdoc researcher will be hired.

Aiming to understand the vulnerability of species and ecosystems to changes and interactions in nutrient availability, stoichiometry, and climate, this project utilizes and analyzes a large and unique dataset of grassland sites across Europe, uses mathematical models of plant communities, and performs experimental tests on critical factors and species interactions to investigate the spatial distribution and diversity patterns of herbaceous plant species and communities over Europe in relation to environmental change. This project is therefore of pivotal importance for developing effective management measures, nutrient policies and network planning for protecting wild populations of plant species in Europe, especially those at risk of extinction. The overarching objective of this project is to identify which types of herbaceous plant communities are most vulnerable to species loss and turnover in response to changes in nutrient supply, nutrient stoichiometry, species invasions, climate change, and their interactions.

Within DiviN-P, the postdoc will work to predict likely future changes in community assembly and diversity patterns in European grasslands, as a consequence of i) expansion of common and invasive species; ii) northward migration of species from Southern Europe because of climate change. Specifically, this work will aim at:
• identifying drivers of current community assembly patterns and functional diversity in different environmental conditions
• delivering scenarios of future changes in alpha and beta diversity due to climate change and to species invasion and expansion

Hiring unit: National Research Council of Italy (Institute of Atmospheric Sciences and Climate, CNR-ISAC, Turin) with Dr. Mara Baudena mara.baudena@cnr.it https://www.isac.cnr.it/en/users/mara-baudena

Expected timeframe of appointment: October 2024 or later (as early as possible is preferable)

Application:
Deadline: August 26th, 2024
Call: https://www.urp.cnr.it/system/files/2024-07/prot_0265986-del-25-07-2024_Bando-ISAC-005-2024-TO_signed.pdf (for non-Italian speakers, apologies! but please feel free to contact mara.baudena@cnr.it)

Shir Bar (shirbar@tauex.tau.ac.il)
2024-08-28 09:25:49

This came across my email, I'm not related to the position:

The faculty of the Department of Biological Sciences at the University of South Carolina, Columbia campus, invites applications for a 9-month, full time, tenure track position at the rank of Assistant Professor to begin on August 16, 2025. We seek to hire a biologist who incorporates artificial intelligence into a research program that addresses fundamental questions in any area of biology. The successful candidate will have a vision for an empirical biological research program in which at least one component substantively uses and/or develops artificial intelligence tools, including but not limited to machine learning, deep learning, neural networks, computer vision, or genetic algorithms.

Candidates must have a Ph.D. in biology or related discipline, and at least one year of postdoctoral research experience in biology by the start date of employment.

The successful candidate will join either our graduate group in Ecology & Evolutionary Biology or our group in Molecular, Cellular, and Developmental Biology (or both) and will be expected to establish an independent, extramurally funded research program relevant to biology. The successful candidate also will be responsible for teaching undergraduate and graduate courses in biology and mentoring undergraduate and graduate students in research.

At the University of South Carolina, we strive to cultivate an inclusive environment that is open, welcoming, and supportive of individuals of all backgrounds. We recognize diversity in our workforce is essential to providing academic excellence and critical to our sustainability. The University is committed to eliminating barriers created by institutional discrimination through accountability and continuous process improvement. We celebrate the diverse voices, perspectives, and experiences of our employees.

The USC Department of Biological Sciences is a multidisciplinary unit including 35 tenured/tenure-track faculty members representing a broad range of research areas. It includes more than 1,800 undergraduate majors and over 70 graduate students and post-doctoral fellows. Our department is complemented by strong research connections to the School of Earth, Ocean, & Environment, the Baruch Institute for Marine and Coastal Sciences, and the USC School of Medicine. For more information about the Department, please visit our website here.

The department is located on the University of South Carolina's main campus in Columbia. The Carnegie Foundation for the Advancement of Teaching has designated the University an institution with "very high research activity." The University has over 35,000 students on the main campus, more than 300 academic programs, and the top Honors College in the United States. Columbia is the center of an increasingly sophisticated metropolitan area with a population of over 800,000, while still maintaining the natural environment through nearby state parks, forests, and Congaree National Park.

The University of South Carolina is an Affirmative Action/Equal Opportunity Institution. Women, minorities, protected veterans, and individuals with disabilities are encouraged to apply. The University of South Carolina does not discriminate in educational or employment opportunities or decisions for qualified persons on the basis of age, ancestry, citizenship status, color, disability, ethnicity, familial status, gender (including transgender), gender identity or expression, genetic information, HIV/AIDs status, military status, national origin, pregnancy (false pregnancy, termination of pregnancy, childbirth, recovery therefrom or related medical conditions, breastfeeding), race, religion (including religious dress and grooming practices), sex, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other bases under federal, state, local law, or regulations.

How to Apply:

All applicants must apply online at USC Jobs at https://uscjobs.sc.edu/postings/174848. Applications must include (1) a curriculum vita, (2) a research statement (up to 3 pages), (3) a statement describing teaching experience and interests (up to 2 pages), and (4) the names and email addresses of at least three references. Letters will be solicited after the initial review of applications by the search committee. Review of applications will begin October 6, 2024, and the position will remain open until filled. Inquiries about the position may be directed to Dr. Jeff Dudycha (dudycha@biol.sc.edu), Search Committee Chair (put "AI Search" in the subject line).

uscjobs.sc.edu
Assistant Professor - Artificial Intelligence Applications in Biology
Original URL: https://uscjobs.sc.edu/postings/174848

Heather Lynch (heather.lynch@stonybrook.edu)
2024-09-12 13:46:30

Stony Brook is hiring an Assistant Professor in AI+Climate: https://apply.interfolio.com/153869

Description: Stony Brook University’s SoMAS and IACS invite applications for a tenure-track Assistant Professor position in artificial intelligence (AI) and/or machine learning (ML) applied to climate and its impacts. The position starts in Fall 2025. We seek candidates addressing climate-related topics through AI/ML from diverse disciplines, such as climate and extreme weather, biogeochemistry, earth systems science, ecology & evolution, and social science. Applicants should have a strong track record of publications in ML applications. The position offers collaboration opportunities within SoMAS, IACS, and the campus's new AI Institute. Candidates should demonstrate strong potential for interdisciplinary collaboration and the ability to establish an externally funded research program. The successful candidate will also engage with faculty in social, natural, and computational sciences, fostering research on the interconnectedness of political, ecological, and social systems. Teaching and mentoring of undergraduate and graduate students are expected, along with contributions to promoting equity, inclusion, and diversity. This tenure-track position has an initial three-year renewable appointment.

Alex Brace (abrace@uchicago.edu)
2024-09-16 13:59:13

Hi all -- Our group is hiring for several exciting projects!

The Ramanathan Lab at Argonne National Laboratory (Lemont, IL near Chicago) is hiring several postdocs for research projects on machine learning/artificial intelligence modeling for various biology-related problems, including protein design, antibody/immunogen design, and large language modeling. We were recently awarded the ACM Gordon Bell Prize for HPC-Based COVID-19 Research for our Genome-Scale Language Models.

We are looking for postdocs with a lot of coding experience (particularly with large language models) – especially those with backgrounds in biophysics, bioinformatics, computational biology, chemical biology, biochemistry, related biological fields, computer science, and/or data science.

We are also hiring postdocs in robotics and related fields to work on Argonne’s Autonomous Discovery initiative.

These positions are open to US and non-US citizens. They will require collaboration with groups local to Chicago, but there is some flexibility in work location. Argonne National Laboratory offers very competitive postdoc salaries (starting around $95,000 in our division), plus benefits.

If you are interested, please email Arvind Ramanathan at ramanathana@anl.gov for more information.

Postdoc opportunity with the Earth Rover Program (with the University of Exeter / UK based) in machine learning for agriculture!

I'm pleased to announce that our organisation Earth Rover Program (ERP) offers a research scientist (postdoc-level) post in machine learning amongst others.

See here for job ad/description, including a form to submit applications:
https://www.earthroverprogram.org/blog/research-positions-in-machine-learning-geophysical-modelling-software-engineering

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/earth-rover-program_ml-geophysical-modellng-software-engineering-activity-7243192995070509056-m3Dl/

Please note the deadline of October 5pm, 2024.

ERP, a non-profit organisation, was founded to develop a rigorous research program for better illuminating, monitoring, understanding and improving soil health in the context of sustainable farming within environmental limits. ERP core team members work in a largely remote environment, with regular meetups in the UK. Both research positions can be remote, or affiliated with the University of Exeter as postdocs.

University of Wyoming is sponsoring a 2 year postdoc fellowship for AI applications related to wildlife in collaboration with their new "WyldTec" initiative. $70k a year and very good shot of strong applications being funded. If you're interested in conducting impactful AI research and applying it to one of the world's great natural ecosystems this is a great opportunity. Lots of scope for collaboration with state, federal, NGO, and private stakeholders. If you have any interest in applying or have questions or want to brainstorm project ideas just reach out to me. I would be happy to serve as an advisor for projects (especially if computer vision related): https://www.uwyo.edu/research/research-at-uw/distinguished-postdoc-fellowship-program.html

UW Distinguished Postdoctoral Fellowships for AI-enabled Interdisciplinary Research
The University of Wyoming is inviting applications for Distinguished Postdoctoral Fellows for conducting convergence research at the intersection of different disciplines. In this year, the program will support a cohort of up to six postdoctoral fellows for two-year terms. Postdoctoral fellows participating in the program will receive professional development opportunities, including entrepreneurship-focused workshops, and mentorship from experts in at least two disciplines. Projects involving collaboration with DOE National Laboratories are encouraged.

Piotr Tynecki (piotr@tynecki.pl)
2024-10-04 04:24:12

I’m not referring to formal rules, but rather the repetitive pattern (in life science projects). I don’t understand why academia and universities aren’t more open to working with freelancers.

Sara Beery (sbeery@caltech.edu)
2024-10-04 04:24:36

Ben Weinstein (benweinstein2010@gmail.com)
2024-10-04 11:21:09

Its really hard to pay you a market wage.

Ben Weinstein (benweinstein2010@gmail.com)
2024-10-04 11:22:59

The universities support those position with other benefits. The market hourly rate for a talented AI engineering role is unsustainable, even for very well funded lab like ours, we've tried. There is also the perception that freelance work is more ephemeral, less likely to be able to form long term relationships

✔️ Jon Van Oast, Tessa Rhinehart
Diego Alejandro Gomez Morales (dieagomezmor@unal.edu.co)
2024-10-04 17:22:33

Totally agree, it's being difficult to find a role having a masters degree. I have found roles that seem a good match for my background but they require PhD.

Aakash Gupta (aakash@thinkevolveconsulting.com)
2024-10-05 03:52:14

It actually feels very exclusionary, when academicians want only PhD candidates or post-docs to apply. Next it will be that Data science jobs are only open for PhD candidates.

Other thing I realized while talking with others academics, is that it might be useful to showcase only PhD candidates while applying for grants. Its easier to showcase a PhD from XYZ University. Rather than a "freelancer" with great credentials but a non-academic background.

Josh Veitch-Michaelis (j.veitchmichaelis@gmail.com)
2024-10-06 13:10:51

There are government funding programs in the UK where companies can get apply for projects that specifically include an academic partner. It's not exactly freelancing, but it's a route for industry to work with academia. I've been on both sides of this and it's a pretty decent system when it works out. From the company side we've built some long-lasting relationships with academic groups, so it's definitely possible.

Another route is an increasing willingness for universities to hire part time/remote staff. I've seen several groups offering this sort of arrangement recently. I've also contracted for national research orgs, so it is possible outside the typical postdoc route. But budget is a big one. There are also plenty of private/non profit conservation orgs that pay closer to market rates, some advertise here as well. There is definitely a skill gap for things like deployment/app development/ops that aren't always best served by (typical) career academics.

Piotr Tynecki (piotr@tynecki.pl)
2024-10-07 02:48:22

@Ben Weinstein I got your point, but not all developers, data scientists or AI researchers are expecting market wages or changing jobs twice a year. I've met many strong candidates for long-term collaboration, but they couldn’t apply due to gaps in their academic careers (non-PhD or non-papers).

For many tech professionals, freelancing (having their own company) is a simply method for handling financial transactions, settlements and gives more flexibility.

This is a fact that the academy grant office / financial departments fails to understand or ignores. The result is that anyone who formally meets the requirements is hired just to kick off the project, with the hope that things will somehow work out later. That's what I observed in last years (from both sites: project manager at University as well as freelancer AI reseacher)

AI to Enhance Ag and Natural Systems - Assistant/Associate/Full Professor - Madison, Wisconsin, United States
Job Summary:<br><br>The Wisconsin Research, Innovation and Scholarly Excellence (RISE) Initiative is designed to help address significant, complex challenges of particular importance to Wisconsin and the world, through accelerated and strategic faculty hiring, research infrastructure enhancement, interdisciplinary collaboration and increased student and educational opportunities. RISE-AI and RISE-EARTH will strengthen the university's ability to address challenges and opportunities in artificial intelligence, translating discoveries into tangible benefits at home in Wisconsin and beyond. In total the RISE hiring initiative will add 20 new faculty members to the College of Agricultural and Life Sciences at UW - <a href="http://Madison.As">Madison.As</a> part of this initiative the...

  • Participation to projects meetings, conferences, etc.

Sara Beery (sbeery@caltech.edu)
2024-10-22 16:12:11

Yet another relevant faculty position!

Assistant or Associate Professor in AI, Biodiversity, and Society

School for Environment and Sustainability

University of Michigan, Ann Arbor


The School for Environment and Sustainability (SEAS) at the University of Michigan invites applicants for a full-time, nine-month, tenure-track Assistant/Associate Professor in Artificial Intelligence, Biodiversity, and Society beginning Fall 2025. The position is part of a two-position cluster hire in Artificial Intelligence for Sustainability using cutting edge technologies to address questions at the intersection of society and the environment. The second position is in AI and Sustainable Systems: http://apply.interfolio.com/156525


We seek a candidate whose teaching and research interests focus on understanding the drivers of change in biodiversity and the implications of those changes for ecosystems and societies, using cutting edge AI techniques to advance their research. Importantly, we are seeking candidates who have an interest in using their research to help solve real-world sustainability challenges. Areas of interest include, but are not limited to, the human influence on biodiversity patterns and processes, biodiversity and ecosystem services, and reciprocal interactions between biodiversity and society. Preference will be given to a candidate that demonstrates the ability to (i) use or develop AI methodologies, (ii) innovatively handle big and heterogeneous datasets to decipher complex patterns, and (iii) address the ethics of AI in their research and teaching.

💙 Jon Van Oast
💚 Angela Zhu
Sara Beery (sbeery@caltech.edu)
2024-10-22 16:12:18

and societal engagement on sustainability. The School focuses on key cross-cutting sustainability themes, including climate and energy; water; food, land use, and agriculture; conservation and restoration; and cities, the built environment, and mobility. There are many opportunities for research collaborations through institutes such as the Michigan Institute for Data & AI in Society and the Institute for Global Change Biology.



The School offers a Ph.D. as well as both research and professional M.S. degrees in seven specializations: ecosystem science and management; geospatial data science; sustainable systems; environmental policy and planning; behavior, education and communication; environmental justice; and sustainability and development. The School also offers a Master’s degree in Landscape Architecture (M.L.A.). The student body includes over 500 M.S./M.L.A. and 60 doctoral students. SEAS also co-manages an undergraduate Program in the Environment and participates in dual master degree programs with the Schools of Architecture and Urban Planning, Business, Engineering, Law, Public Health, and Public Policy. Additional information about the School can be found at www.seas.umich.edu.



The successful candidate will be expected to:

Develop a widely recognized research program that attracts external funding and contributes to the interdisciplinary mission of the School;

Support SEAS's teaching mission at both graduate and undergraduate levels, including mentoring and supervising doctoral and master’s students;

Contribute to the stewardship of SEAS, the University, relevant professions and society through service, collaboration and engagement.

The position will be filled at the assistant professor (tenure-track) or associate professor (tenured) level.



Applications should include (1) a cover letter; (2) CV; (3) a concise personal statement describing your vision and plans for research, teaching and societal engagement; (4) a one-page statement that showcases a demonstrated commitment to diversity efforts, and (5) a list of three names of academic references with contact information.



To apply, submit application materials in Interfolio, via this link: http://apply.interfolio.com/156521



Review of applications will begin on November 15, 2024 and continue until the position is filled. SEAS hopes to appoint a faculty member to this position to begin Fall 2025.



At SEAS we are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and equitable environment that respects diverse experiences, promotes generous listening and communications, and discourages and restoratively responds to acts of discrimination, harassment, or injustice. Our commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion is deeply rooted in our values for a sustainable and just society.

The University of Michigan is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer.

Adriaan Dokter (amd427@cornell.edu)
2024-10-28 15:38:07

Postdoc position at Cornell Lab of Ornithology in Ithaca, NY

BirdFlow is an innovative, NSF-funded project that uses the Cornell Lab’s eBird database to infer the movements of migratory bird populations across the annual cycle. We are looking for a quantitative ecologist that will lead research that utilizes BirdFlow models to expand our knowledge of bird movements, including studying migratory connectivity and its associations with population trends, investigating species’ resilience to climate change and environmental fluctuations, and exploring BirdFlow models for providing real-time information on species’ movements in the context of the BirdCast public website. You will also have an opportunity to work with additional data sources, such as BirdCast radar data, individual tracking data and banding data, to improve and validate BirdFlow models. Our team includes a diverse group of ornithologists and computer scientist, bringing together researchers at Cornell Lab, UMass Amherst and University of Illinois at Urbana Champaign. In addition to working on exciting ecological research, you will be able to contribute to products and software that will be released for scientists, conservation practitioners, industry, and the public, broadening the impact of your research. This position is preferably in person at the Lab of Ornithology in Ithaca NY. We are looking for someone to start as soon as possible, options exist for a hybrid work schedule.

Official Job listing: https://academicjobsonline.org/ajo/jobs/27782
Last Date to Apply: Oct 31 2024

😎 Jon Van Oast, Robin Zbinden
🐦 Oisin Mac Aodha, Angela Zhu

Heather Lynch (heather.lynch@stonybrook.edu)
2024-11-06 15:45:19

New faculty position in AI and Climate at Stony Brook University: https://apply.interfolio.com/153869

Stony Brook University’s SoMAS and IACS invite applications for a tenure-track Assistant Professor position in artificial intelligence (AI) and/or machine learning (ML) applied to climate and its impacts. The position starts in Fall 2025. We seek candidates addressing climate-related topics through AI/ML from diverse disciplines, such as climate and extreme weather, biogeochemistry, earth systems science, ecology & evolution, and social science. Applicants should have a strong track record of publications in ML applications. The position offers collaboration opportunities within SoMAS, IACS, and the campus's new AI Institute. Candidates should demonstrate strong potential for interdisciplinary collaboration and the ability to establish an externally funded research program. The successful candidate will also engage with faculty in social, natural, and computational sciences, fostering research on the interconnectedness of political, ecological, and social systems. Teaching and mentoring of undergraduate and graduate students are expected, along with contributions to promoting equity, inclusion, and diversity. This tenure-track position has an initial three-year renewable appointment.

🔈 Oisin Mac Aodha
❤️ Andy Viet Huynh, FANQI Z, Millie Chapman

The Conservation Technology Lab at the San Diego Zoo Wildlife Alliance has an opening for a post-doc from computer science or a related field with strong experience in machine learning to work on bioacoustics projects in ecology and conservation. The projects have global impact including in our conservation programs in the Peruvian Amazon, Cameroon's Ebo Forest, the savannas of Kenya, our Southern Californian grasslands, and the Arctic.

We have interesting problems and we have interesting data. Lots of both actually!

Scroll down to the "What We Are Looking For" section of the link below (there is a lot of boilerplate elsewhere in the call that I feel might be confusing) to see more specifics about the position:

Hi all,
We are hiring an intern for for Summer 2025.

AI2 is seeking a passionate early-career AI engineer/researcher to help build the next generation of AI for Earth. We are developing a cutting-edge platform for planetary modeling, monitoring and forecasting, leveraging massively multi-modal foundation models to address diverse use cases across land use, conservation, sustainability, climate change, energy, and beyond.

You will collaborate with research scientists and AI engineers to design and build innovative models that harness massive datasets from remote sensing, in situ observations, weather systems, and climate simulations. These models will be deployed in real-world products and applications to create tangible impacts on the ground.

Previous AI interns on this team have published award-winning papers, been recognized by AI2’s prestigious “outstanding internship award”, and gone on to esteemed careers in ML and AI.

https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/thealleninstitute/jobs/6450256

Tiziana Gelmi Candusso (tiziana.gelmi@gmail.com)
2025-01-17 10:52:51

Postdoctoral Position in Wildfire Science and LiDAR at the University of Toronto

The James Lab (www.jameslab.ca) at the University of Toronto is looking for a collaborative, enthusiastic, and creative post-doctoral researcher to contribute to cutting-edge projects in fuels assessment using lidar to improve fire risk modelling and mitigation.

At the JamesLab, you will be at the forefront of forest disturbance ecology and wildfire science, working on research that informs risk mitigation strategies and advances ecosystem management. With access to advanced computational resources and collaborations spanning academia, government, industry, and first nations, this role provides a broad range of opportunities to develop novel solutions to global challenges. Based in Toronto, a vibrant and multicultural city, you will join a collaborative and well-supported research team committed to scientific excellence.

The position will be part of a large inter-disciplinary project focused on reducing fire risk through better fuels characterization. The successful candidate will have the opportunity to interact with university researchers, provincial and federal government scientists, as well as other students and post-docs from across Canada who are also examining questions related to disturbance ecology, fire behaviour, lidar, remote sensing, and fire / forest management. We are specifically looking for a highly technical and conceptually competent researcher to take a leadership role in this large multi-faceted project.

Qualifications

  • Completed PhD in Forestry, Wildfire Science, Remote Sensing, Computer Science, or related field

  • Strong expertise in statistics, high-performance computing, GIS, and remote sensing

  • High level programming proficiency (e.g., Python, R)

  • Demonstrated experience with LiDAR (TLS, ALS) data collection and processing (e.g., lidR, Computree, CloudCompare)

  • Excellent oral and written English communication skills

  • Familiarity with wildfire behaviour models, simulation modelling, fuel mapping, and/or fire risk assessment

  • Previous post-doctoral experience will be considered an asset

Funding will be competitive and commensurate with qualifications (salary range: $65K-$80K). Up to three years of funding is available for this position. Support will also be provided for field work, professional development, and conference travel. The anticipated start date is May 2025. More information about post-doctoral positions at the University of Toronto can be found here: https://postdoc.sgs.utoronto.ca/

To apply, please send a short letter of motivation outlining how you meet the qualifications above, your CV, recent transcripts, and the names of three academic references to Dr. Patrick James: patrick.james@utoronto.ca --

❤️ Owen Martin
Oisin Mac Aodha (macaodha@caltech.edu)

Hi all, I’m the co-founder & CEO of Plantaer (www.plantaer.com), a startup developing new materials that integrate plant life (currenly working with photosynthetic organisms and epiphytes) directly into urban surfaces, including roofs, walls, and infrastructure barriers (basically everything except windows and pavement).
We’re looking for a core team member to help build our data collection systems (hardware and software), develop computer vision algorithms, and create species distribution models. Compensation will depend on experience, availability, and alignment with our mission. If you’re interested or know someone who might be a great fit, feel free to message me directly or email me at manuel@plantaer.com.

Research Scientist working at the intersection of wildlife and machine learning at University of Wyoming. 3 years of initial funding but is expected to be a long term research position. Initially work with my lab and Wyoming Game and Fish to develop aerial pronghorn monitoring systems to deploy across the American West and generally help build stuff as part of the emerging "WyldTech" community. Note, two positions are being hired, but one is specifically for wildlife related applications (deadline March 2): https://eeik.fa.us2.oraclecloud.com/hcmUI/CandidateExperience/en/sites/CX_1/job/243604/?la[…]c%253BClassified+Staff%253BAdministrative%252FExecutive

Leonie Hodel (leoniejhodel@gmail.com)
2025-03-07 18:04:33

Hi everyone! I wanted to share this great research fellowship opportunity in Madison, USA. If this sounds interesting to you, feel free to reach out to me or Holly Gibbs -- I’d be happy to answer any questions! I’ve worked on these projects during my PhD and postdoc, so I can provide some insights😊 🤗

The Global Land Use and Environment Lab (www.gibbs-lab.com) at the University of Wisconsin-Madison seeks nominees for a post-doctoral research fellowship program with links to Land System Science, applied econometrics, data science, and spatial AI. Our lab uniquely combines novel AI-based technology with conservation solutions in the Brazilian Amazon, and application of the technology in collaboration with key stakeholders on the ground.
- For our initiative to map individual cattle in the Amazon, we are particularly seeking candidates with an interdisciplinary background with experience in spatial analysis, computer vision and deep learning-based models. This effort could also include commodity supply chain mapping to reduce deforestation.
- Beyond this, we develop AI-based models to link together camera trap data, soundscapes, and VHR satellite imagery-based maps of cattle stocking densities to asses Jaguar poaching risk and occurrence, biodiversity trends, and animals movement data to plan forest restoration.
- Additionally, we are leveraging 30cm satellite data to analyze the intensification of Brazil’s cattle sector over time, evaluating its impacts on greenhouse gas emissions, land use, and human health.
If the proposal is funded, the post-doc research fellowships pay $75K in salary and come with some research support. Distinguished Research Fellows nominated for this award must anticipate defending their Ph.D. by June 2025 or be no more than two years into a postdoctoral position.
Please send questions or interest to Professor Holly Gibbs: hkgibbs@wisc.edu

Patrick Beukema (patrickb@allenai.org)
2025-03-18 19:22:10

Hi all!

I am hiring for multiple roles (AI scientists/engineers) for a new series initiatives at Ai2.

We are building a new open platform, called earth-system, centered on large-scale mixed-modality planetary modeling to address some of our planet’s most pressing challenges: climate change, food security, sustainability and conservation, wildfire management, and beyond. You can read a bit more about the platform here: https://allenai.org/earth-system

You will join an exceptionally capable AI team. We are deeply collaborative team (research and engineering and product++, within and outside of Ai2) and we are strongly committed to open source AI research and development. You will see our team’s work at many upcoming AI conferences this year (ICLR, ICML, CVPR), as well as several specialized conferences (e.g. ESA-NASA Workshop on AI Foundation Models). Here is some recent press from NVIDIA covering one of our recently released models for maritime intelligence and wildlife applications (GPS transformers): https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/ai-model-offers-conservationists-new-tools-to-protect-fisheries-wildlife-at-scale/

Apply here: https://lnkd.in/gauQ_7Mj

Please feel free to reach out if you believe you can advance this agenda even if your skills are not a precise match for the role!

NVIDIA Technical Blog
AI Model Offers Conservationists New Tools to Protect Fisheries, Wildlife at Scale | NVIDIA Technical Blog
In an effort to rein in illicit fishing, researchers have unveiled a new open-source AI model that can accurately identify what virtually all of the world’s seafaring vessels are doing…
Original URL: https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/ai-model-offers-conservationists-new-tools-to-protect-fisheries-wildlife-at-scale/

Emily Lines (erl27@cam.ac.uk)
2025-03-24 02:24:49

I am hiring! Do you like data and forests? Enjoy both computational work and fieldwork?

I am searching for a Computational and Field Senior Technician to join my UKRI FLF project team for 24 months, based in the University of Cambridge, UK (not remote). The role will focus on collection and processing of TLS and UAV data in forests, to improve our understanding of how 3D structure relates to forest function, diversity and dynamics in Europe. More details here (ad closing April 7th): https://www.jobs.cam.ac.uk/job/50700/

The role is open to UK and international candidates. Candidates do not necessarily need to be someone with a PhD - though could - this might well suit a strong Masters or BSc student who is keen on getting research experience.

I'm keen to find someone with strong computational (python as a minimum) skills and, if they do not yet have experience, a clear interest and enthusiasm for fieldwork/outdoor work. Send me an email or DM for informal enquiries.

Please share!

Sara Beery (sbeery@caltech.edu)
2025-03-31 17:11:53

Postdoc and Intern positions in Panama:

"The Muller-Landau Lab at the Smithsonian Tropical Research Institute (STRI) in Panama is seeking postdoctoral scholars to advance our understanding of tropical woody plant communities through quantitative analyses and/or theory development drawing on large datasets for tropical forests in Panama.
The postdocs will undertake research applying or elaborating mathematical theory to understand mechanisms of coexistence and/or relative abundances of woody plant species in tropical forests. Within this broad area, there is substantial flexibility to choose research questions and methods that align with the postdoc’s interests and expertise.

Candidates need not have prior experience working in tropical forests. Candidates should have strong quantitative skills and prior experience with applying complex quantitative analyses, theory, or modeling to address ecological questions. Start date is flexible, and funding from the Simons Foundation is available for up to three years.

To apply, email your curriculum vitae, a cover letter describing research interests and experience, a first-authored publication or manuscript, and contact information for three references to Helene Muller-Landau at mullerh@si.edu. Review of the initial set of applications will begin April 21, 2025; later applications will be considered as they arrive contingent on available funding. Informal inquiries are welcome. More information at https://hmullerlandau.com/contact/


Interns

I’m currently recruiting interns to participate in several research projects: quantifying landscape-level variation in tropical forests , automated species classification of tropical plants, and evaluating the climate sensitivity of tropical tree demographic rates. Internships typically extend for 9-12 months, with flexible start dates. All interns have the opportunity to gain experience in tropical forest field data collection, data management and data cleaning using R and GitHub, statistical analyses and figure preparation using the R programming language, reviewing scientific literature, scientific discussions in English, working in a team with lab members and collaborators from diverse backgrounds, preparation of scientific presentations, preparation of data publications, and the scholarly publishing process. The ideal candidates have a bachelor’s degree in a relevant field, strong quantitative skills including programming experience, strong English oral and written communication skills, at least basic Spanish oral communication skills, and the ability and motivation to lead or co-lead a scholarly publication.

The positions are particularly well suited for candidates seeking more research experience prior to graduate school. Each internship is compensated with a modest stipend sufficient to cover living expenses in Panama (US$1250/month), as well as roundtrip travel to Panama in the case of non-Panamanian candidates.

To apply, email your curriculum vitae, a cover letter in English succinctly describing your qualifications and why you are interested in this internship, a scientific writing sample (e.g., undergraduate thesis, term paper, lab report), a commented sample of code you have written (preferably in R or Python), and an unofficial copy of your undergraduate transcript to Helene Muller-Landau at mullerh@si.edu. More information at https://hmullerlandau.com/contact/"

Quantitative Forest Ecology
Join The Lab
I welcome thoughtful inquiries from prospective postdocs, interns, and graduate students interested in joining the lab. Postdocs Simons-funded I’m currently recruiting postdocs to advance our…
Original URL: https://hmullerlandau.com/contact/

Victor Boussange (vic.boussange@gmail.com)
2025-04-08 05:39:19

📢 We are looking for a talented master student in ML/DL to work at the intersection of scientific machine learning and ecology.

🌱 We propose a master thesis aiming at forecasting invasive species expansion using graph and physics-informed neural networks.

Invasive species impose massive economic costs, exceeding $423 billion annually, and are a major threat for biodiversity. 🫵 This is your opportunity to use your skills to make a significant societal impact.

📍You are preferably based in Zürich, Switzerland; no funding is available for this position.

You’ll benefit from a close supervision from Konstantinos Tatsis and Michele Volpi (Swiss Data Science Center) and myself (Swiss Federal Research Institute for Forest, Snow and Landscape), great computing resources, and the chance to publish your work in a top-tier ML conference.

See full details here.

📧 Interested? Drop me a message!

💚 Negar Sadrzadeh

I just learned that this was a channel! Reposting in case anybody missed it earlier, as the deadline is approaching:

📣 Seeking postdoctoral researcher: Bridging AI and wildlife ecology 📣
The Gaynor Lab at the University of British Columbia :flag_ca: is recruiting a postdoctoral researcher to lead a self-directed project that bridges the fields of AI and wildlife ecology. This position is supported by the AI for Biodiversity Change (ABC) Global Center, which brings together ecologists and computer scientists from six universities in the United States and Canada to develop new AI-enabled, data-supported approaches for understanding the impacts of environmental changes on biodiversity (https://www.abcresearchcenter.org/).

Sara Beery (sbeery@caltech.edu)
2025-04-10 16:40:25

New RFP From TNC:

"The Nature Conservancy is seeking a doctoral or post-doctoral researcher to help us understand opportunities to leverage artificial intelligence for the conservation of freshwater biodiversity.

The primary objective of this short-term project is to assess the potential of artificial intelligence (AI) for applications related to monitoring status and changes in freshwater biodiversity & freshwater ecosystems. With emphasis on approaches that enable monitoring at watershed-scales—including within low resource contexts of the Global South—this research project aims to provide a roadmap for TNC to prioritize applications and partnerships for AI for freshwater biodiversity conservation.

The attached RFP describes the specific activities and deliverables to support the stated objective. However, we also welcome additional/alternative approaches that may better achieve the primary objective.

We are seeking to receive proposals by April 30, 2025."

Rita Pucci (rita.pucci85@gmail.com)
2025-04-16 13:13:06

One open Phd position at Leiden Institute of Advanced Computer Science (LIACS) in Leiden, Netherlands.

"TATTOOS: paTtern formATion Trough biO-inspired cOmputer viSion".

Our goal is to develop a cutting-edge deep neural network that harnesses advanced insights into pattern formation systems found in animal skin. This innovative network will empower us to accurately identify individual animals based on their distinct markings. You will have the unique opportunity to collaborate in a dynamic and interdisciplinary environment, working alongside leading experts in computer vision and imaging at LIACS (Leiden, NL), groundbreaking researchers in mathematical mechanistic models at Vrije University (Amsterdam, NL), and renowned biologists specializing in skin patterns formation at Naturalis (Leiden, NL). Together, we will push the boundaries of research and make a significant impact in the field.

https://careers.universiteitleiden.nl/job-invite/15628/

Tanya Berger-Wolf (berger-wolf.1@osu.edu)
2025-04-29 14:39:47

Summer Employment Opportunities with CTY 
Zoology

 
Greetings! 
 
The Johns Hopkins University Center for Talented Youth (CTY) is seeking Instructors and Teaching Assistants for our summer programs. CTY offers challenging academic programs for academically advanced elementary, middle, and high school students from across the country and around the world. Positions are available at residential colleges and universities in East and West coast locations.
 
Why Teach Science for CTY? 

  • Engage with highly motivated students who love learning 

  • Teach what you love – lead exciting, advanced courses in science

  • Small class sizes – each class has an instructor and a teaching assistant 

  • Collaborate with dedicated educators from around the world 

  • Competitive salary, plus housing and meals provided at all residential positions

  • Professional development – gain valuable teaching experience in a supportive, collegial environment 

 
Science Courses Offered 
We are currently seeking instructors for courses such as: 
  • Zoology

 
For a full list of CTY courses available, visit: CTY Summer Program Courses 
 
Program Locations & Dates 
  • Residential sites: Bristol, RI. and Los Angeles, CA.

  • Session 1: June 19 – July 12 | Session 2: July 12 – August 2 

  • RELOCATION ASSISTANCE MAY BE AVAILABLE


 
Who We’re Looking For 
  • Science educators, professors, graduate students, or specialists with experience teaching or mentoring students 

  • Instructors will lead challenging, college-level courses for 14-20 students 

  • Instructor Salary: $3,944 – $4,869 per session (plus room & board !!) 

  • Teaching Assistant Salary: $1,825.95 per session


 
Apply Now! 
➡️ Fill out an application here: Apply Now 
🎓 Hiring is rolling – apply early for the best opportunities! 
 
 

Help Spread the Word! 
Please share this opportunity with colleagues, students, and fellow educators who may be interested. 
📩 Questions? Contact ctyrecruiting@jhu.edu 
We hope to welcome you to CTY’s math faculty this summer! 
 


CTY Recruiting, Human Resources 
Johns Hopkins Center of Talented Youth

jobs.jhu.edu

2025 CTY On-Campus Summer Programs Employment

Sara Beery (sbeery@caltech.edu)
2025-04-30 17:27:25

From Alyosha, mentions biodiversity!

Attn: recent computer vision PhD graduates and postdocs. If you prefer not to watch the self-destruction of American science, this is a nice junior faculty opportunity at ENPC near Paris (where I am spending my sabbatical).

Main features:
- paid significantly more than standard french academic position
- include a starting package covering approximately 1PhD + 2-3 years post-doc / 2PhDs
- no teaching load
- it's 4 years but of course we hope to make it permanent (should be easy if the person gets a big grant or ends up leading many projects)
- it's more targeted to someone who would already have done a postdoc, but a really great PhD student, from a big university, with a convincing project could potentially make it
- timeline is short: talk to us asap, write an application by May 31st, interview in June, start in September - October
- topic should be "Computer Vision for X", with X being something that's relevant to the school, but this is quite broad, e.g., biodiversity, environment, robotics, architecture...

❤️ Justin Kay, Subhransu Maji, Jason Holmberg (Wild Me), Jennifer, Jon Van Oast, Alan Stenhouse, Reaz Masud

Tanya Berger-Wolf (berger-wolf.1@osu.edu)
2025-05-07 14:57:12

Postdoc in data science for ecology. Please contact the lab directly (I am just reposting):
INTERDISCIPLINARY POST-DOCTORAL SCIENTIST POSITION
(IN ECOSYSTEM DATABASE DEVELOPMENT & HUMAN DIMENSIONS)
The Ohio State University – Aquatic Ecology Laboratory
Description: Dr. Stuart Ludsin (https://u.osu.edu/ludsinlab/) is seeking a post-doctoral scientist to help on a long-term project designed to advance the use of ecosystem-based approaches to fisheries management in the North American Great Lakes Basin. The post-doc would be expected to conduct literature reviews, organize workshops, and implement surveys of database managers/developers, fishery managers, and academic and agency researchers, to help identify a data-sharing architecture (“database ecosystem”) suitable for use in the Great Lakes Basin. A potential need to conduct structured or semi-structured interviews also exists. Ideally, this database ecosystem would allow end-users (e.g., academic and agency researchers, fishery managers) to simultaneously query independent Great Lakes databases and download their needed data through a user-friendly online interface.
In addition to writing and submitting manuscripts for publication to peer-reviewed journals, the post-doc would be expected to present findings at management and scientific meetings, as well as help generate annual progress reports to the Great Lakes Fishery Commission, which is this project’s sponsor. The opportunity to contribute to ongoing fisheries-focused research in the Great Lakes basin, including refining and using an independent Great Lakes Recruitment and Ecosystem (GLaRE) database, would also exist, if of interest.
Funding exists for two years with an ideal start time being summer 2025.
Qualifications: Required qualifications include: (1) a PhD in social science, data science, computer science, information systems, environmental science, ecology, or related field; (2) demonstrated ability to conduct independent research, as evidenced by peer-reviewed publications; and (3) strong written and oral communication skills, including the ability and desire to interact with Great Lakes stakeholders and database managers/developers from diverse international organizations. While knowledge of database development and associated programming could prove beneficial, it is not required.
Salary: $61,008 - $63,000 annually, depending on experience, plus a comprehensive benefits package.
Location: Successful candidates would join a dynamic, interactive group of faculty, post-docs, and students at the Aquatic Ecology Laboratory (AEL; http://ael.osu.edu/) in the Department of Evolution, Ecology, and Organismal Biology (http://eeob.osu.edu/) on The Ohio State University’s main (Columbus) campus. Computing support and office space would be available in the AEL. Working remotely is NOT possible.
How to apply: Submit to apps.ael@gmail.com in a single .pdf file: 1) a cover letter highlighting your research background and interests; 2) a full CV; and 3) names and contact information of three references. Please include “Database Postdoc” and your last name in the subject line.
Application review will begin May 19th and continue until a suitable candidate is found. Questions about the position can be addressed to Stuart Ludsin (ludsin.1@osu.edu).
The Ohio State University is an equal opportunity employer, including veterans and disability.

🐟 Sara Beery, Oisin Mac Aodha, Matthew Walker (he/they), Joseph Dimos, Inês Nolasco, Alan Stenhouse

Sara Beery (sbeery@caltech.edu)
2025-06-20 08:23:58

Google DeepMind's AnthroKrishi team, focused on innovative and impactful solutions at the intersection of agriculture, remote sensing, and AI (see https://agri.withgoogle.com/), is offering a 6-month Student Researcher internship. This role involves research and development of machine learning/computer vision models to derive key insights from satellite, aerial, and other remote sensing data, with the aim of addressing real-world challenges in agriculture.

Required Qualifications/Skills:

  • Should be currently enrolled in a PhD program in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science or a related quantitative field

  • Demonstrated ability to conduct independent research with publications in top-tier ML/CV venues (such as ICML, NeurIPS, ICLR, AAAI, CVPR, ICCV)

  • Strong programming proficiency in Python and experience working with common data manipulation, scientific computing and ML frameworks and libraries (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, Pandas, NumPy).

  • Understanding of machine learning concepts and the lifecycle of ML models, including deployment and inference challenges.

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills focused on system performance and scalability.

  • Ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a collaborative team.

  • Good communication skills.

Preferred:
  • Experience in ML models for remote sensing or geospatial data.

  • Knowledge of remote sensing, climate change, agricultural science concepts.

  • Experience with geospatial data processing tools and libraries (GDAL, Rasterio, Shapely, PostGIS, etc.).

agri.withgoogle.com
Agricultural Understanding platform
A repository of Google's work in agriculture and remote sensing
Original URL: https://agri.withgoogle.com/

Joseph Dimos (joedimos@gmail.com)
2025-06-24 14:00:40

Hello! I’m currently on the hunt for a direct contract role (if possible) concerning some intersections with ML and object tracking that focusses on insect classification. My direct involvement here, however is with ML combined with some physics-informed approaches in plume navigation synthesis of camera trap data. My background coincides with the clarification of physics problems associated to dynamics in various types of turbulent environments, thus solving Navier-Stokes for various types of turbulent flow. I’m adept in deep learning in conjunction with ST-GNNs and multi-agent tracking. I’d be more than happy to share a PoC/CV that highlights some of these directions while providing more granularity. My primary email is joedimos@gmail.com

Matt Ziegler (mattzig@cs.washington.edu)
2025-07-02 17:54:21

My Ocean Nexus colleague Gerald Singh is hiring a postdoc in Interactive Simulations and Serious Games for Climate Adaptation at University of Victoria:

Hi all,

I'm recruiting for a postdoc to use interactive simulation and serious games to plan for climate adaptation strategies robust to extreme uncertainty with management agencies and coastal communities. This position will help create new methods and processes for inserting people into simulation-based analysis to capture human voice, creativity and problem solving where classic projection-based models cannot.

I'm looking for someone with good background in workshop facilitation, community engagement, and/or computer-human interaction and simulation-based modeling.

I'm very excited about this direction of research. If you know of anyone who would be a good fit please forward the ad (attached) to them.

Gerald

Jean-Olivier IRISSON (jean-olivier.irisson@imev-mer.fr)
2025-07-07 16:21:07

Hi everyone,
Another call for interested parties for this 2y post-doc opportunity on the French Riviera. The goal is to develop deep learning solutions for the prediction of the distribution of plankton, planktonic particles and tropical fishes; this work will therefore be at the interface between ecology and AI. We are looking for applicants trained in computer sciences with an interest in ecology but would also welcome applications from biology PhDs with significant experience in deep learning.
https://emploi.cnrs.fr/Offres/CDD/UMR7093-MADWAL-021/Default.aspx?lang=EN
The work will be carried out in a dynamic team of about 50 people, with a significant number of PhD students/post-docs and five PIs. Called "Computational plankton ecology", the team is well embedded in many international programs and renowned for its breakthroughs in plankton imaging and the analysis of the large resulting datasets. The lab is in a historical building, by the Mediterranean Sea, which makes working there all the more enjoyable 😉.
Don't hesitate to ask if you want more details about the position.
Bye,

👏 Malte Pedersen
❤️ Matthias Zuerl, Josh Seltzer, Alan Stenhouse

Do you want your postdoc to drive real-world change? The Smithsonian Institution and The Nature Conservancy Sustainability Fellowship offers six early-career scientists the chance to lead high-impact, interdisciplinary research at the intersection of climate, conservation, and community resilience.

🌱 Research themes include:
• Nature-based health solutions in Kenya
• Climate-smart agroforestry in Brazil
• Blue carbon science in global wetlands

🗺️ Two tracks. Apply according to your eligibility:
🔹 Based in the U.S. or authorized to work in the U.S.?
👉 Apply through the Smithsonian:
● Postdoctoral Fellow (Agroforestry): https://lnkd.in/e7Y9GkMC
● Postdoctoral Fellow (Water Funds Program): https://lnkd.in/ebftwaSa
● Postdoctoral Fellow (Blue Carbon Initiative): https://lnkd.in/e5tGnvBp

🔹 Based outside the U.S. and authorized to work in Kenya, Brazil or other TNC partner countries?
👉 Apply through The Nature Conservancy:
● Postdoctoral Fellow (Agroforestry):https://lnkd.in/eSdXYmsC
● Postdoctoral Fellow (Water Funds Program): https://lnkd.in/eshqkixV
● Postdoctoral Fellow (Blue Carbon Initiative): https://lnkd.in/e-mf8_5R

📆 Applications open July 3 | Due July 31

💬 Join our Info session on July 14, 1:00 - 2:00 pm EDT. Sign-up here: https://lnkd.in/eWb6VAD9

The Bat Conservation Trust are looking to recruit an Ecoacoustics Support Officer for three months with an immediate start. The role will support the management, classification and processing of audio recordings through our cloud-based Sound Classification System during the busy summer and post-summer period. Full details are included in the attached role description.

If you would like to apply for this position, please reply to Katherine Boughey at kboughey@bats.org.uk by 17:00 on 29th July with

  • Your CV

  • A short cover letter explaining how your skills and experience meet the requirements of the Person Specification

  • Your earliest start date (we will take availability into account when offering this position).

  • Contact details of two references


We can only employ candidates who have the Right to Work in the UK and we will conduct the necessary checks before employment begins. Please do not apply if you do not have the Right to Work it the UK/valid immigration status. We will hold a short interview during the week commencing 4th August 2025. Please let us know if you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process.

The Marine Innovation Lab for Leading-edge Oceanography (MILLO) based in the Department of Ocean and Resources Engineering (ORE) at the School of Ocean and Earth Science and Technology (SOEST), University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa is actively recruiting 1-2 MS/PhD students for Fall 2026.

We are prioritizing students whose research interests fall within one of the following areas:

  • artificial intelligence (AI) for marine conservation,

  • edge computing in wildlife behavior and ecology,

  • underwater acoustics, and

  • ocean instrumentation.


Please read the documents attached for more details about the available projects, background of ideal candidates and application process.

Please complete the interest form on our website: https://pagniello-lab.github.io/join-us.html. Email inquiries will not be returned.

Oisin Mac Aodha (macaodha@caltech.edu)
2025-08-14 07:58:54

Research Fellow in Machine Learning, School of Computer Science, University of St Andrews
The School of Computer Science advertises a Research Fellow position in machine learning. We are looking for an enthusiastic person with a PhD degree in computer science (or last-year PhD student), with background and experience in deep neural networks, preferably with expertise in any of the following subfields: signal processing and transformers.

Key Duties and Responsibilities:
1. Develop a deep learning-based call detector for gibbons
2. Analyse example data set and report the results
3. Support the integration of the detector into PAMGuard software
4. Co-develop user-manual
5. Manuscript for publication

Informal enquiries can be directed to Dr. Cornelia Oedekoven (cso2@st-andrews.ac.uk) and Dr. Juan Ye (juan.ye@st-andrews.ac.uk).

❤️ Robin Zbinden
Puneet Kollipara (kollipython@gmail.com)
2025-08-21 15:16:08

As part of this year's FOSS4G North America, slated for November 3–5 in Reston, VA (see forwarded post for details), we'd like to invite all folks seeking or hiring for geospatial/GIS jobs or training the next generation of geospatial professionals to connect with one another over a delicious treat at our first-ever Geo-Career Expo & Ice-Cream Mixer! Geospatial/GIS job seekers, hirers, recruiters, and trainers/educators alike, even those not attending FOSS4G NA 2025 itself, are welcome at this free event, slated for November 3 from 1:00 to 3:00 PM. Separate registration is required; simply submit this short form.

Please spread the word to your students, colleagues and associates. If you are a hiring manager, recruiter, admissions officer, or representative of a training/upskilling organization and you have questions, or if you are interested in sponsorship and partnership opportunities, please send me a DM.

Puneet Kollipara (https://aiforconservation.slack.com/team/U06U6QSKXAN)
<p><a href="https://web.cvent.com/event/5a9e1809-2ffb-484c-8998-4289fcb4199e/register?RefId=FOSS4GNA2025Registration">Early-bird registration is underway</a> for this year's <strong><a href="https://foss4gna.org">FOSS4G North America</a></strong>, the premier North American conference on open geospatial technologies! Taking place in <strong>Reston, VA</strong> (near Washington, DC) on <strong>November 3–5</strong>, FOSS4G NA 2025 will explore geospatial open-source software, open data, open science, open collaboration, Earth observation / remote sensing (EO/RS), GeoAI, mapping, and more. Check out the <a href="https://www.foss4gna.org/schedule">full schedule</a> of informative talks and hands-on workshops, and our assortment of <a href="https://www.foss4gna.org/social">activities and socials</a>.<br><br><strong>HIGHLIGHTS</strong><br><strong>Keynote lineup:</strong> We're thrilled that <strong><a href="https://www.foss4gna.org/speakers/katie-picchione">Katie Picchione</a></strong> of <strong>NASA's Disasters Response Coordination System</strong> will deliver one of three top-tier keynote talks. Katie uses her cross-cutting background to get EO/RS data and info into the right hands during disasters. Also keynoting FOSS4G NA 2025: <strong><a href="https://www.foss4gna.org/speakers/bruce-momjian">Bruce Momjian</a></strong> (EnterpriseDB and PostgreSQL Global Development Group) and <strong><a href="https://www.foss4gna.org/speakers/paul-ramsey">Paul Ramsey</a></strong> (Crunchy Data / Snowflake), two leading figures in the geospatial OS software and data-infrastructure landscape.<br><br><strong>For job seekers and hirers/trainers:</strong> The first-ever <strong><a href="https://www.foss4gna.org/social#career">Geo-Career Expo &amp; Ice-Cream Social</a></strong>! We invite geo-job seekers and hiring/training org members to forge connections over an ice-cream treat the first day. It's <em><strong>FREE</strong></em> and <em><strong>open to ALL</strong></em>. No conference badge is needed, but <a href="https://forms.gle/mtMXWHwuJbjf6aRm8">separate registration</a> is required for both job seekers and hirers/trainers.<br><br><strong>Get involved and spread the word!</strong> As a conference dedicated to open-source communities, we invite everyone to play a role in FOSS4G NA 2025. Here's how:<br>• <strong>Present a poster:</strong> You have until <em><strong>August 26</strong></em> to <a href="https://talks.osgeo.org/foss4g-na-2025/cfp">submit your poster ideas</a>!<br>• <strong>Become a sponsor:</strong> Expand your organization’s reach by supporting open geospatial ecosystems. Read our <https://static1.squarespace.com/static/6660a51220c4d544be347123/t/681a9e723bd3be22bb7ccbf5/1746574962919/FOSS+2025+Exhibitor+%26+Sponsor+Brochure+FINAL.pdf|sponsorship prospectus> for details.<br>• <strong>Volunteer:</strong> Enjoy <em><strong>FREE</strong></em> general admission in exchange for <a href="http://foss4gna.org/volunteer">volunteering your time</a> to help FOSS4G NA run smoothly.<br>• Above all, <em><strong>please spread the word among your colleagues and networks!</strong></em><br>Act by <em><strong>September 17</strong></em> to lock in early-bird savings. Feel free to DM me with questions. See you in November!</p>

Google Docs
Geo-Career Expo and Ice Cream Mixer Registration
Join us for the Geo-Career Expo &amp; Ice Cream Mixer on Monday, November 3rd, from 1:00–3:00 PM. Whether you're actively job hunting, hiring, or just curious about the geospatial job market, this free and informal event is your chance to connect with others in the open source geospatial community.<br><br>Please visit the FOSS4G NA website to learn more about this and other FOSS4GNA events.

Jean-Olivier IRISSON (jean-olivier.irisson@imev-mer.fr)
2025-08-29 10:13:53

Hi,
We're still looking for applicants to this post-doc
https://emploi.cnrs.fr/Offres/CDD/UMR7093-MADWAL-022/Default.aspx?lang=EN
It seems that posting job adverts over the summer was not very successful so we're trying again now.

The goal is to develop deep learning solutions for the prediction of the geographic distribution of plankton, planktonic particles and tropical fishes; this work will therefore be at the interface between ecology and AI. We are looking for applicants trained in computer sciences with an interest in ecology but would also welcome applications from biology PhDs with significant experience in deep learning.

The work will be carried out in a dynamic team of about 50 people, comprising several collaborating PIs and many students and post-docs. Called "Computational plankton ecology", the team is well embedded in many international programs and renowned for its breakthroughs in plankton imaging and the analysis of the large resulting datasets. The lab is in a historical building, by the Mediterranean Sea, which makes working there all the more enjoyable 😉

Don't hesitate to ask if you want more details about the position.
Bye,

🎉 Jon Van Oast, Shir Bar

The Environmental Data Science Innovation and Impact Lab (ESIIL) within CIRES at the University of Colorado Boulder is currently hiring our third cohort of Postdoctoral Research Scholars who are working in the field of environmental data science (EDS) to address pressing environmental challenges. ESIIL postdocs are expected to produce innovative, applied environmental biology research while also building a broad community to thrive in the rapidly evolving field of EDS.
